channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
item.description: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.
item.content:encoded:
Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Trump heads to China for state visit. And, how the war in Iran has affected inflation
item.description: Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The economic chilling effect of Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.
item.content:encoded:
New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: U.S. ambassador to Israel says Israel sent Iron Dome batteries, personnel to UAE
item.description: Israel sent Iron Dome anti-missile batteries and personnel to operate them to the United Arab Emirates to defend the country during the Iran war, the U.S. ambassador to the country said Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Israel sent Iron Dome anti-missile batteries and personnel to operate them to the United Arab Emirates to defend the country during the Iran war, the U.S. ambassador to the country said Tuesday.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 24 hours with 3 teenage birders: Welcome to the World Series of Birding
item.description: The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.
item.content:encoded:
The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.

(Image credit: Mohamed Sadek for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Escobar

item.title: The clipping economy: How short-form video 'clippers' are overrunning the internet
item.description: Short-form clips of long interviews and shows are taking over the internet. But behind the sea of social media clips are marketplaces offering freelance clippers money per view.
item.content:encoded:
Short-form clips of long interviews and shows are taking over the internet. But behind the sea of social media clips are marketplaces offering freelance clippers money per view.

(Image credit: Emrah Bayraktar)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Trump goes to China as Iran war smolders
item.description: President Trump says he has a great relationship with President Xi. His trip to China will provide a temperature check.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says he has a great relationship with President Xi. His trip to China will provide a temperature check.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: As Trump goes to China, what do Americans say about tariffs, Iran and world standing?
item.description: Most Americans see China as one of the United States' biggest rivals or adversaries, but largely as an economic threat, according to a new Chicago Council/NPR/Ipsos poll.
item.content:encoded:
Most Americans see China as one of the United States' biggest rivals or adversaries, but largely as an economic threat, according to a new Chicago Council/NPR/Ipsos poll.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Drive 5 hours or fly 20 minutes? Remote towns suffer from lack of year-round flights
item.description: A federal program that pays airlines to operate in small and rural communities could have its budget cut in half, leaving parts of the country with no flight options.
item.content:encoded:
A federal program that pays airlines to operate in small and rural communities could have its budget cut in half, leaving parts of the country with no flight options.

(Image credit: Agata Storer for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer

item.title: She's trying to outrun pancreatic cancer. Breakthrough treatments give her hope
item.description: Pancreatic cancer is notoriously lethal. But new treatments mean that may be changing, and people with the disease now have more reason to hope than ever before.
item.content:encoded:
Pancreatic cancer is notoriously lethal. But new treatments mean that may be changing, and people with the disease now have more reason to hope than ever before.

(Image credit: Gavin McIntyre for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi
